title: Terminal API Integration - Cluster Selection Flow
description:
Implement cluster selection and kubeconfig resolution for terminal access.

**User Flow**:
1. User selects a cluster from their available clusters
2. Frontend retrieves cluster_id (cluster name) and uuid (KrknTargetRequest UUID)
3. Terminal component receives these values
4. All commands executed against selected cluster until user switches

**API Parameters Mapping**:
- cluster_id: The 'cluster-name' field from KrknTargetRequest.Status.TargetData
- uuid: The KrknTargetRequest.Spec.UUID value
- Both are available from GET /api/v1/clusters?id=<uuid>

**Implementation**:
1. Add cluster selector dropdown/list in terminal UI
2. Fetch available clusters via existing clusters API
3. Store selected cluster_id and uuid in component state
4. Pass these values to every terminal API call
5. Display selected cluster name in terminal header
6. Allow cluster switching without losing command history

**Permissions**:
- Users need 'view' or 'edit' permission on cluster
- Backend validates permissions, but frontend can pre-filter list
- Admin users see all clusters

**Error Handling**:
- Handle 403 Forbidden if user lacks permission
- Handle 404 Not Found if cluster/uuid not found
- Show clear error message to user

**Reference**: See TERMINAL_API.md 'Permissions' section

title: Reload provider config every time Provider Configuration tab is accessed
description:
**Problem:**
Provider configuration is loaded only once. After saving, the backend deletes the config CR, so saving multiple attributes without reloading causes exceptions.

**Requirements:**
EVERY time the user clicks on the "Provider Configuration" tab (eventKey 2), re-execute ALL API calls:
1. Reset provider config state to idle
2. Create new provider config request
3. Trigger schema polling

**Current Code (Settings.tsx lines 66-93):**
```tsx
if (
  activeTabKey === 2 &&
  state.providers && 
  state.providers.length > 0 &&
  state.providerConfigStatus === 'idle'  // ← Prevents reload!
)
```

**Solution:**
1. Track previous activeTabKey with useRef
2. When activeTabKey changes TO 2:
   - Dispatch PROVIDER_CONFIG_RESET action
   - Create new config request (even if status not idle)

**Files to Modify:**
- src/components/Settings.tsx
- src/context/AppContext.tsx (add PROVIDER_CONFIG_RESET if needed)

**Testing:**
1. Open Provider Configuration tab → loads
2. Save a config
3. Switch to another tab
4. Return to Provider Configuration → fresh reload

title: Frontend: User CRUD API - Architecture Overview
description:
# User Management API Overview

The backend has implemented a complete REST API for user management. This guide documents API behavior for frontend implementation.

## Endpoint Structure

**Base path**: `/api/v1/users` (NOT /api/v1/auth/users)

**Authentication**: All endpoints require JWT token in header:
```
Authorization: Bearer <token>
```

## Available Endpoints

1. **GET /api/v1/users** - List users (admin only)
2. **GET /api/v1/users/:userId** - Get user details (admin or self)
3. **POST /api/v1/users** - Create user (admin only)
4. **PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId** - Update profile (admin or self)
5. **DELETE /api/v1/users/:userId** - Delete user (admin only)
6. **PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password** - Change password (admin or self)

## ⚠️ CRITICAL: Two Different PATCH Endpoints

There are TWO different PATCH endpoints for the same user:

1. `PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId` - Updates PROFILE (name, surname, org, role, active)
2. `PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password` - Changes PASSWORD

**Do NOT confuse them!** Password is NOT updated via profile PATCH.

## Permission Matrix

| Action | Admin | User (self) | User (others) |
|--------|-------|-------------|---------------|
| List users | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| View user | ✅ | ✅ (own only) | ❌ |
| Create user | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Update name/surname/org | ✅ | ✅ (own only) | ❌ |
| Update role/active | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Delete user | ✅ | ❌ (not even self) | ❌ |
| Change password | ✅ | ✅ (own only) | ❌ |

## Backend Security Rules

1. **Cannot delete self**: Even admin cannot delete their own account
2. **Cannot delete last admin**: Backend prevents deleting the last active admin
3. **Current password required**: Users must provide current password to change it
4. **Admin bypass**: Admin does NOT need current password to change other users' passwords

## Frontend Implementation Required

Create:
- UserService for API calls
- Permission guards/checks based on role
- Separate forms for update profile vs change password
- Client-side validation matching backend rules
- Error handling for all cases (403, 404, 409, etc.)

## Related Tasks

See individual implementation tasks for each endpoint.

title: Frontend: User CRUD API - Overview e Architettura
description:
# Overview API User Management

Il backend ha implementato un'API REST completa per la gestione utenti. Questa guida documenta il comportamento dell'API per l'implementazione frontend.

## Struttura Endpoints

**Base path**: `/api/v1/users` (NON /api/v1/auth/users)

**Authentication**: Tutti gli endpoint richiedono JWT token nell'header:
```
Authorization: Bearer <token>
```

## Endpoints Disponibili

1. **GET /api/v1/users** - Lista utenti (admin only)
2. **GET /api/v1/users/:userId** - Dettagli utente (admin or self)
3. **POST /api/v1/users** - Crea utente (admin only)
4. **PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId** - Aggiorna profilo (admin or self)
5. **DELETE /api/v1/users/:userId** - Elimina utente (admin only)
6. **PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password** - Cambia password (admin or self)

## ⚠️ IMPORTANTE: Due Endpoint PATCH Diversi

Ci sono DUE endpoint PATCH per lo stesso user:

1. `PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId` - Aggiorna PROFILO (nome, cognome, org, role, active)
2. `PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password` - Cambia PASSWORD

**NON confonderli!** La password NON si aggiorna con il PATCH del profilo.

## Permission Matrix

| Azione | Admin | User (self) | User (others) |
|--------|-------|-------------|---------------|
| List users | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| View user | ✅ | ✅ (solo proprio) | ❌ |
| Create user | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Update name/surname/org | ✅ | ✅ (solo proprio) | ❌ |
| Update role/active | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Delete user | ✅ | ❌ (neanche se stesso) | ❌ |
| Change password | ✅ | ✅ (solo propria) | ❌ |

## Regole di Sicurezza Backend

1. **Non può cancellare se stesso**: Anche admin non può cancellare il proprio account
2. **Non può cancellare ultimo admin**: Backend impedisce di eliminare l'ultimo admin attivo
3. **Password corrente richiesta**: User deve fornire password corrente per cambiarla
4. **Admin può cambiare password altrui**: Admin NON serve password corrente per altri utenti

## Implementazione Frontend

Creare:
- UserService per chiamate API
- Permission guards/checks basati su role
- Form separati per update profilo vs change password
- Validazione client-side che rispetti le regole backend
- Error handling per tutti i casi (403, 404, 409, etc.)

## Task Correlati

- Frontend: Implementare lista utenti (GET /users)
- Frontend: Implementare form crea utente (POST /users)
- Frontend: Implementare form modifica profilo (PATCH /users/:id)
- Frontend: Implementare form cambia password (PATCH /users/:id/password)
- Frontend: Implementare elimina utente (DELETE /users/:id)

title: File Types Management UI - Create and edit file types
description:
Create UI for admins to create and edit file type metadata (color only).

Create Form Features:
- Name field (required, validated as Kubernetes label-compatible)
- Color picker (hex color, optional - defaults to UI default if empty)
- Preview of badge with selected color
- Name is used as ID and cannot be changed after creation
- Validation: unique name, valid hex color format (#RRGGBB)

Edit Form Features:
- Cannot change name (read-only)
- Can update color only
- Show current usage count (how many files use this type)
- Preview badge with changes
- Clear color button to reset to default (send empty string)

API endpoints:
- POST /api/v1/file-types (create new type, admin only)
  Request: { name, color }
- PUT /api/v1/file-types/{name} (update color, admin only)
  Request: { name, color }
- GET /api/v1/file-types/{name} (get current type)
  Response: { name, color, usageCount, createdAt }

Admin only feature.

Note: Icon field removed from spec - only color customization available.
notes:
Auto-Created Types Editing:

IMPORTANTE: Admin può editare tipi auto-creati per aggiungere color/icon!

Use Cases:
1. Creazione manuale (prima di usarlo):
   - Admin crea tipo "config" con color="#00FF00" e icon="file-code"
   - Poi users creano files con questo tipo
   
2. Editing tipo auto-creato (più comune):
   - User crea file con tipo "script" (auto-creation, no metadata)
   - Admin vede tipo "script" in lista con color="" e icon=""
   - Admin edita tipo per aggiungere color="#FF5733" e icon="terminal"
   - Tutti i files con tipo "script" vedono badge aggiornato

Edit Form Details:
- Name field: read-only (cannot change, it's the identifier)
- Color field: 
  - Se vuoto: mostra "Using UI default color"
  - Color picker per scegliere hex color
  - "Clear" button per tornare a default (salva "")
- Icon field:
  - Se vuoto: mostra "Using UI default icon"
  - Icon selector/input per scegliere
  - "Clear" button per tornare a default (salva "")
- Usage count: mostra quanti file usano questo tipo
- Preview badge: mostra come appare con color/icon selezionati

Validation:
- Color: deve essere hex format #RRGGBB o vuoto
- Icon: string libero o vuoto
- Name: immutabile, già validato alla creazione

API per edit:
PUT /api/v1/file-types/{name}
{
  "name": "script",  // must match URL, backend ignores this
  "color": "#FF5733",  // or "" for default
  "icon": "terminal"   // or "" for default
}
close_reason: Implemented as FileTypesManagementModal accessible from RUN dropdown. All features completed: list with previews, create/edit forms with color picker, delete with usage validation, search/filter.

title: Frontend: Implement Update User Profile (PATCH /users/:id)
description:
# Update User Profile Endpoint

**Endpoint**: `PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId`

**⚠️ IMPORTANT**: This endpoint updates profile fields ONLY. For password, use separate endpoint `PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password`

## Authorization

- **Admin**: Can update any user (all fields)
- **User**: Can update own profile only (name, surname, organization)
- **User cannot update**: role, active (admin only fields)

## Request

**Method**: `PATCH` (partial update - send only fields you want to change)

**Headers**:
```
Authorization: Bearer <token>
Content-Type: application/json
```

**Body** (all fields optional):
```json
{
  "name": "Updated Name",
  "surname": "Updated Surname",
  "organization": "New Organization",
  "role": "admin",      // Admin only
  "active": true          // Admin only
}
```

## Response

**Success (200)**:
```json
{
  "message": "User updated successfully",
  "user": {
    "userId": "user@example.com",
    "name": "Updated Name",
    "surname": "Updated Surname",
    "organization": "New Organization",
    "role": "user",
    "active": true,
    "created": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z",
    "lastLogin": "2025-01-20T15:30:00Z"
  }
}
```

## Error Responses

**400 - Validation Error**:
```json
{
  "error": "validation_error",
  "message": "At least one field must be provided"
}
```

```json
{
  "error": "validation_error",
  "message": "Role must be either 'user' or 'admin'"
}
```

**403 - Forbidden**:
```json
{
  "error": "forbidden",
  "message": "You can only update your own profile"
}
```

```json
{
  "error": "forbidden",
  "message": "Only admins can change role or active status"
}
```

**404 - Not Found**:
```json
{
  "error": "not_found",
  "message": "user with email 'user@example.com' not found"
}
```

## Frontend Implementation

### User Profile Edit Form

**For regular users**:
- Show fields: name, surname, organization
- Hide/disable: role, active (read-only display)
- Can only edit own profile

**For admins**:
- Show all fields: name, surname, organization, role, active
- Can edit any user

### Validation

Client-side validation:
- At least one field must be changed
- Role must be "user" or "admin" if provided
- Prevent users from changing role/active (hide/disable inputs)

### Example Implementation

```typescript
async function updateUserProfile(userId: string, updates: Partial<UserProfile>) {
  const response = await fetch(`/api/v1/users/${userId}`, {
    method: 'PATCH',
    headers: {
      'Authorization': `Bearer ${token}`,
      'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    },
    body: JSON.stringify(updates)
  });
  
  if (!response.ok) {
    const error = await response.json();
    throw new Error(error.message);
  }
  
  return await response.json();
}

// Usage - Update own profile
await updateUserProfile('me@example.com', {
  name: 'New Name',
  surname: 'New Surname'
});

// Usage - Admin updates user role
await updateUserProfile('user@example.com', {
  role: 'admin',
  active: true
});
```

## Testing Scenarios

1. User updates own name/surname - should succeed
2. User tries to update own role - should fail 403
3. User tries to update other user - should fail 403
4. Admin updates any user's role - should succeed
5. Admin updates user's active status - should succeed
6. Update with empty body - should fail 400
7. Update non-existent user - should fail 404

## UI/UX Considerations

- Show different form fields based on user role
- Disable role/active fields for non-admins
- Show success message after update
- Refresh user data after successful update
- Handle errors gracefully with user-friendly messages
- Consider "Save" button state (disabled until changes made)

## Related Endpoints

- **GET /api/v1/users/:userId** - Get user details before editing
- **PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password** - Separate endpoint for password (DO NOT CONFUSE)

title: Terminal API Integration - Implement Command Validation
description:
Add client-side validation to prevent blocked commands before sending to backend.

**Why**: Improve UX by catching errors early instead of waiting for backend rejection.

**Blocked Flags** (streaming, not supported in v1):
- --watch, -w
- --follow, -f  
- --watch-only

**Blocked Commands** (write operations):
- apply, create, delete, edit, patch, replace, scale, etc.

**Allowed Commands** (read-only):
- get, describe, logs, top, explain, version, api-resources, api-versions, cluster-info

**Implementation**:
1. Parse command string to extract subcommand and flags
2. Check if subcommand is in allowed list
3. Check if any blocked flags are present
4. Show user-friendly error message before API call

**Example Error Messages**:
- 'Streaming commands (--watch, --follow) are not supported in this version'
- 'Write operations (delete, apply, create) are not permitted'
- 'Only read-only kubectl commands are allowed'

**Bonus**: Add auto-completion suggestions for allowed commands

**Backend Validation**: Backend will still validate, this is just for better UX.

title: Implement user file permissions for create/update/delete operations
description:
## Context

Backend has implemented role-based permissions for file operations. Users can now create, update, and delete files (not just admins), but with restrictions based on group membership.

## New Permission Model

### Admin
- Can create files for any group or as public
- Can update any file
- Can delete any file

### User (non-admin)
- Can create files for **their own groups** or as public
- Can update files from **their own groups** or public files
- Can delete files from **their own groups** or public files
- **Cannot** create/update/delete files for groups they don't belong to

## API Endpoints Behavior

### POST /api/v1/files (Create)
- **Authentication required** (admin or user)
- Users can create files with:
  - `availableToAll: true` (public file)
  - `groups: ["their-group"]` (file for their own group)
- Backend validates:
  - User must belong to the group they're assigning
  - Returns **400 Bad Request** if user tries to assign to other groups
  - Returns **400 Bad Request** if both `groups` and `availableToAll` are set

### PUT /api/v1/files/{name} (Update)
- **Authentication required** (admin or user)
- Users can only update files they have access to:
  - Files from their own groups
  - Public files
- Backend returns **403 Forbidden** if user tries to update files from other groups

### DELETE /api/v1/files/{name} (Delete)
- **Authentication required** (admin or user)
- Same access rules as update:
  - Users can delete files from their own groups
  - Users can delete public files
- Backend returns **403 Forbidden** if user tries to delete files from other groups

### GET /api/v1/groups (List Groups)
- **Role-based filtering** (already implemented):
  - Admin: returns all groups
  - User: returns only groups the user belongs to
- Frontend can use this to populate group selection in file forms

### GET /api/v1/files/{name} (Get File)
- **Role-based access** (already implemented):
  - Admin: can read any file
  - User: can only read files they have access to (own groups or public)
- Used for pre-populating edit forms

## Frontend Implementation Requirements

### File Creation Form
1. **Group Selection Dropdown**:
   - Fetch groups from `GET /api/v1/groups` (automatically filtered by role)
   - Admin sees all groups, user sees only their groups
   - Add "Public (Available to All)" option
   - Make group and public mutually exclusive (checkbox/radio pattern)

2. **Validation**:
   - Ensure either a group is selected OR "public" is checked (not both)
   - Handle 400 Bad Request errors with user-friendly messages

### File Edit Form
1. **Pre-population**:
   - Use `GET /api/v1/files/{name}` to fetch current file data
   - Pre-select the current group or "public" option

2. **Permission Checks**:
   - If user tries to edit a file they don't have access to, backend returns 403
   - Show appropriate error message: "You don't have permission to edit this file"

### File List/Table
1. **Action Buttons** (Edit/Delete):
   - For users: show edit/delete buttons only for files they have access to
   - For admins: show all action buttons
   - Consider disabling buttons instead of hiding (better UX)

2. **Access Indicators**:
   - Show file group badge or "Public" badge
   - Help users understand which files they can modify

### Error Handling
- **400 Bad Request**: Validation error (e.g., trying to assign to a group user doesn't belong to)
  - Message: "You can only assign files to your own groups"
- **403 Forbidden**: Permission denied (e.g., trying to edit file from another group)
  - Message: "You don't have permission to modify this file"
- **500 Internal Error**: Show generic error, log details

## Example Scenarios

### User "alice@example.com" (belongs to "dev-team")

**Can do:**
- Create file with `groups: ["dev-team"]`
- Create file with `availableToAll: true`
- Edit file from "dev-team"
- Edit public files
- Delete file from "dev-team"
- Delete public files

**Cannot do:**
- Create file with `groups: ["ops-team"]` → 400 Bad Request
- Edit file from "ops-team" → 403 Forbidden
- Delete file from "ops-team" → 403 Forbidden

### Admin User

**Can do:**
- All operations on all files
- Create files for any group
- Edit any file
- Delete any file

## Backend Validation Summary

The backend already handles all permission validation:
- `ValidateUserFilePermissions()` checks group membership
- `canAccessFile()` verifies user has access for read/update/delete
- Frontend doesn't need to duplicate these checks, just handle responses

## Testing

Frontend should test:
1. User can create public file
2. User can create file for own group
3. User cannot create file for other group (handles 400 error)
4. User can edit file from own group
5. User cannot edit file from other group (handles 403 error)
6. User can delete file from own group
7. User cannot delete file from other group (handles 403 error)
8. Admin can do all operations

## References

Backend implementation: krkn-operator commits:
- `ff360e0`: fix(api): allow users to create/update/delete files for their own groups
- `42ca0be`: feat(api): implement file types system with auto-creation and role-based permissions

title: File Types Management UI - Delete file type with usage validation
description:
Create UI for admins to delete file types with usage validation.

Features:
- Delete button in file type list and detail view
- Before deleting, check usage count
- If usage count > 0: show warning modal with list of files using this type
  - Error message: 'Cannot delete type X - used by N files'
  - Show list of file names using this type (from backend error message)
  - Suggest removing the type from all files first
  - No delete action possible
- If usage count = 0: show confirmation dialog
  - Warning: 'Delete file type X?'
  - Confirmation button to proceed
- On successful delete, redirect to file types list
- Handle 409 Conflict error from backend (type in use)

API endpoint:
- DELETE /api/v1/file-types/{name} (admin only)
  - Returns 409 Conflict with file list if type is in use
  - Returns 200 OK if deleted successfully

Backend prevents orphaned files by rejecting deletion when type is in use.

Admin only feature.
notes:
NO Auto-Deletion - Manual Cleanup Required:

IMPORTANTE: I file types NON vengono auto-cancellati, anche se non più usati!

Background:
- Backend auto-crea tipi quando servono
- Ma NON auto-cancella tipi quando l'ultimo file smette di usarli
- Questo è intenzionale per evitare cancellazioni accidentali
- Admin deve cancellare manualmente i tipi inutilizzati

Workflow:
1. User crea file con tipo "temp" → auto-creation
2. User cancella il file o cambia tipo
3. Tipo "temp" rimane con usage count = 0
4. Admin vede tipo "temp" in lista (usage: 0 files)
5. Admin può cancellarlo manualmente se vuole

Delete Protection:
- Backend rifiuta DELETE se usage count > 0
- Errore 409 Conflict con lista file che usano il tipo
- Frontend mostra lista file e blocca eliminazione
- User deve prima rimuovere tipo da tutti i file

UI Flow:
1. Admin clicca delete su tipo con usage > 0:
   - Modal: "Cannot delete - used by N files:"
   - Lista file names
   - No delete button, solo "Close" o "View files"
   
2. Admin clicca delete su tipo con usage = 0:
   - Confirmation: "Delete file type X? This action cannot be undone."
   - Conferma → DELETE /api/v1/file-types/{name}
   - Success → redirect to list

Optional Feature:
- "Orphaned types" filter: mostra tipi con usage count = 0
- Bulk delete per cleanup di tipi inutilizzati

title: Fix scenarioImage construction for private registry scenarios
description:
## Problem

When launching a scenario from a private registry, the frontend constructs scenarioImage incorrectly by hardcoding 'krkn-hub:' prefix:

```typescript
const scenarioImage = `krkn-hub:${scenarioName}`;  // ❌ Wrong for private registry
```

This causes the backend to construct an invalid image path:
```
quay.io/rh_ee_tsebasti/krkn-hub-private:krkn-hub:node-cpu-hog  ❌
```

Instead of the correct path:
```
quay.io/rh_ee_tsebasti/krkn-hub-private:node-cpu-hog  ✅
```

## Root Cause

File: `src/components/ScenarioDetail.tsx`

The component always prefixes scenario names with 'krkn-hub:', which is only correct for the default public registry (`quay.io/krkn-chaos/krkn-hub`).

For private registries, the scenarioImage should be just the tag name.

## Solution

Distinguish between public and private registry scenarios:

```typescript
// Get registry information
const isPrivateRegistry = selectedRegistry && selectedRegistry.name !== 'default';

let scenarioImage: string;
if (isPrivateRegistry) {
  // Private registry: use only the tag name
  scenarioImage = scenarioName;  // e.g., 'node-cpu-hog'
} else {
  // Public registry: use full image path with krkn-hub prefix
  scenarioImage = `krkn-hub:${scenarioName}`;  // e.g., 'krkn-hub:node-cpu-hog'
}
```

## Expected Behavior

**For public registry (default):**
- scenarioImage: `krkn-hub:node-cpu-hog`
- Backend constructs: `quay.io/krkn-chaos/krkn-hub:node-cpu-hog`

**For private registry:**
- scenarioImage: `node-cpu-hog`
- Backend constructs: `quay.io/rh_ee_tsebasti/krkn-hub-private:node-cpu-hog`

## Files to Modify

- `src/components/ScenarioDetail.tsx` - Main fix location
- `src/components/ScenarioDetail.test.tsx` - Update tests

## Testing

1. Test with default public registry - should still work
2. Test with private registry (quay-private) - should now pull correct image
3. Verify image pull succeeds in pod events
close_reason: Fixed in commit 77bdd23. ScenarioImage now correctly built without krkn-hub prefix for private registries.

4. User selects one or more groups (required, at least one)

- Groups field is required

Two approaches:

**Option A (Recommended): Sequential API calls**

- If user creation fails: abort, show error
- If user created but group add fails:
  - User exists but not in all groups

title: Terminal API Integration - Create API Client Service
Frontend: Password change uses separate endpoint
description:
# Password Change Behavior

⚠️ **IMPORTANT**: Password changes use a SEPARATE endpoint from profile updates.

## Two Different PATCH Endpoints

When updating a user, there are TWO different PATCH endpoints:

### 1. Update Profile (PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId)
- Updates: name, surname, organization, role*, active*
- Does NOT update password
- Use this for profile edits

### 2. Change Password (PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password)
- Updates: password only
- Does NOT update profile fields
- Use this for password changes

## Password Change Request Body

**User changing own password** (requires current password):
```json
{
  "currentPassword": "OldPass123",
  "newPassword": "NewPass456"
}
```

**Admin changing other user's password** (no current password needed):
```json
{
  "newPassword": "NewPass456"
}
```

## Key Differences from Profile Update

1. **Different URL**: Append `/password` to the user URL
2. **Different body**: Uses currentPassword/newPassword fields, not profile fields
3. **Different validation**: Requires current password for self (not for admin)
4. **Cannot mix**: Cannot send password field to profile endpoint or vice versa

## Implementation Notes

- Create separate form/modal for password change
- Do NOT add password field to profile edit form
- Check if user is admin to show/hide currentPassword field
- Validate password strength client-side (min 8 chars, uppercase, lowercase, number)
- Show success message after password change
- Consider forcing re-login after password change

## Example API Calls

```typescript
// WRONG - This will NOT change password
PATCH /api/v1/users/user@example.com
{
  "name": "New Name",
  "password": "NewPass123"  // ❌ Ignored!
}

// CORRECT - Profile update
PATCH /api/v1/users/user@example.com
{
  "name": "New Name",
  "surname": "New Surname"
}

// CORRECT - Password change
PATCH /api/v1/users/user@example.com/password
{
  "currentPassword": "OldPass123",
  "newPassword": "NewPass456"
}
```

notes:
EDIT FORM - PRE-COMPILAZIONE DATI:

**API per ottenere dati attuali del file:**

```
GET /api/v1/files/{name}
```

**Permessi:**
- Admin: può leggere qualsiasi file
- User: può leggere solo file a cui ha accesso (pubblici o del suo gruppo)
- Se user non ha accesso: 403 Forbidden

**Response (FileResponse):**
```json
{
  "name": "my-config",
  "fileName": "config.yaml",
  "content": "{\"key\": \"value\"}",
  "mountPath": "/etc/config",
  "description": "My configuration file",
  "fileType": "configuration",
  "groups": ["team-a"],
  "availableToAll": false,
  "createdAt": "2026-06-10T10:00:00Z",
  "createdBy": "admin@example.com",
  "updatedAt": "2026-06-10T11:00:00Z",
  "updatedBy": "user@example.com"
}
```

**Flow Edit Form:**

1. User clicca "Edit" su file
2. Frontend fa GET /api/v1/files/{name}
3. Pre-compila form con dati ricevuti:
   - fileName input ← response.fileName
   - content editor ← response.content
   - mountPath input ← response.mountPath
   - description textarea ← response.description
   - fileType dropdown ← response.fileType
   - groups/public radio ← response.groups / response.availableToAll
4. User modifica campi
5. Submit PUT /api/v1/files/{name} con nuovi valori

**Gestione Errori:**

Se GET restituisce 403 Forbidden:
- User non ha accesso al file
- Mostrare messaggio: "You do not have access to this file"
- Non permettere edit

Se GET restituisce 404 Not Found:
- File non esiste (potrebbe essere stato cancellato)
- Redirect a lista file con messaggio

**Pre-compilazione Groups/Public:**

Se `response.availableToAll === true`:
- Selezionare radio "Public file"
- Groups dropdown disabled/empty

Se `response.groups.length > 0`:
- Selezionare radio "Assign to group" (admin) o "My group file" (user)
- Pre-selezionare `response.groups[0]` nel dropdown

title: [TECHNICAL] File Permissions Display - Show access level indicators
description:
Add visual indicators to show file permission levels in the file list/detail views.

**Context:**
Files can be:
- Public (availableToAll=true)
- Group-restricted (groups=[single-group])

Users can only edit files assigned to their own group or public files.
Admins can edit any file.

**Display Requirements:**

**File List View:**
Each file should show permission badge:
- Public file: Badge "Public" (green) or icon 🌍
- Group file: Badge "Group: team-a" (blue) or icon 🔒 + group name

**File Detail View:**
Show permission section:
- "Access: Public - Available to all users"
- "Access: Group - Only team-a members"

**Edit/Delete Buttons:**
- Admin: always enabled for all files
- User: 
  - Enabled if file is public
  - Enabled if file is assigned to user's group
  - Disabled if file is assigned to other group (show tooltip: "You cannot edit files from other groups")

**API Data:**
From FileResponse:


**User Groups:**
Fetch user's groups from: GET /api/v1/users/{userId}
Response includes user's group memberships.

**Permission Check (Client-side):**


**UI Examples:**

File List:
| Name        | Type   | Permission      | Actions |
|-------------|--------|----------------|---------|
| config.yaml | config | 🌍 Public      | Edit Delete |
| secret.json | config | 🔒 team-a      | - (disabled if not in team-a) |

Detail View:
notes:
FILE PERMISSION DISPLAY - Technical Implementation

**1. Permission Badges in List:**

Public file:
- Badge: "Public" (green background)
- Icon: 🌍 or similar
- Tooltip: "Available to all users"

Group file:
- Badge: "Group: team-a" (blue background)
- Icon: 🔒 + group name
- Tooltip: "Only team-a members can access"

**2. Edit Button Enablement:**

```javascript
function canEditFile(file, userGroups, isAdmin) {
  // Admin can edit anything
  if (isAdmin) return true;
  
  // Public files can be edited by anyone
  if (file.availableToAll) return true;
  
  // Group files: user must belong to the group
  if (file.groups.length > 0) {
    return userGroups.includes(file.groups[0]);
  }
  
  // Neither public nor group-assigned (shouldn't happen)
  return false;
}
```

**3. Get User Groups:**

Option A: From user profile endpoint
GET /api/v1/users/me or GET /api/v1/users/{userId}

Option B: From auth context (if available in JWT claims)

Response should include user's group memberships:
```
{
  "userId": "user@example.com",
  "role": "user",
  "groups": ["team-a", "team-b"]
}
```

**4. Visual Examples:**

File List Table:
```
Name          | Type   | Access         | Actions
------------- | ------ | -------------- | -------
config.yaml   | config | 🌍 Public      | [Edit] [Delete]
secrets.json  | config | 🔒 team-a      | [Edit] [Delete] (if in team-a)
other.yaml    | script | 🔒 team-b      | 🚫 (disabled, tooltip: "You cannot edit files from other groups")
```

File Detail View:
```
📄 config.yaml

Type: configuration
Access: 🌍 Public - Available to all users
Mount: /etc/config
Created: 2026-06-10 by admin@example.com

[Edit Button - enabled] [Delete Button - enabled]
```

```
📄 secrets.json

Type: config  
Access: 🔒 Group - Only team-a members
Mount: /secrets
Created: 2026-06-10 by user@example.com

[Edit Button - disabled if user not in team-a]
  Tooltip: "You cannot edit files assigned to team-a (you belong to: team-c)"
```

**5. FileResponse Structure:**

```json
{
  "name": "config.yaml",
  "fileName": "config.yaml",
  "content": "...",
  "mountPath": "/etc/config",
  "fileType": "configuration",
  "groups": [],
  "availableToAll": true,
  "createdBy": "admin@example.com",
  "createdAt": "2026-06-10T10:00:00Z"
}
```

If groups is empty and availableToAll is true → Public file
If groups has 1 element and availableToAll is false → Group file

**6. Error Handling:**

If user tries to edit file they don't have access to:
- Client should prevent action (disabled button)
- If bypassed: server returns 403 Forbidden

Display server error message from validation:
"you can only assign files to your own group (you belong to: [team-a])"

title: [REFERENCE] File Types Auto-Creation Pattern - Frontend Implementation Guide
description:
Reference guide for implementing file types with auto-creation pattern in the frontend.

## Auto-Creation Pattern Overview

File types use GitHub-style label pattern: types are auto-created when first used.

### Backend Behavior

**Auto-Creation (on file create/update):**
- User creates file with fileType: "config"
- Backend checks if KrknFileType "config" exists
- If NOT exists: creates it with name="config", color=""
- If exists: reuses existing type
- File gets label: file-type.krkn.krkn-chaos.dev/config=true

**NO Auto-Deletion:**
- Removing type from last file does NOT delete the type
- Types persist even with usage count = 0
- Admin must manually delete unused types
- DELETE with usage > 0 returns 409 Conflict

### Frontend Implementation

**File Create/Edit Forms:**
1. Fetch types: GET /api/v1/file-types
2. Show combobox with existing types + freeform input
3. User can:
   - Select existing type (shows with color badge)
   - Type new name (freestyle, auto-created on save)
   - Leave empty (file without type)
4. On submit: just send fileType field, backend handles creation

**Do NOT:**
- ❌ Call POST /api/v1/file-types before creating file
- ❌ Validate if type exists before sending
- ❌ Disable freeform input

**Do:**
- ✅ Allow freeform text input for new types
- ✅ Show existing types with metadata (color badge preview)
- ✅ Provide link to "Manage Types" for customization
- ✅ Handle color="" gracefully (use UI defaults)

**File Types Management:**
1. List shows all types (auto-created + manually configured)
2. Auto-created types have empty color (use defaults)
3. Admin can edit any type to add color
4. Delete blocked if usage count > 0 (show file list)
5. Delete allowed if usage count = 0

### Example Flow

1. Dev creates file:
   POST /api/v1/files { fileType: "config" }
   → Backend auto-creates type "config" (no metadata)

2. Admin sees type in list:
   GET /api/v1/file-types
   → Returns: { name: "config", color: "", usageCount: 1 }

3. Admin customizes type:
   PUT /api/v1/file-types/config { color: "#00FF00" }
   → All files see updated badge

4. Dev removes type from file:
   PUT /api/v1/files/myfile { fileType: "" }
   → Type "config" still exists (usage: 0)

5. Admin cleans up:
   DELETE /api/v1/file-types/config
   → Success (usage = 0)

### API Endpoints

**Read (all users):**
- GET /api/v1/file-types → list all
  Response: { fileTypes: [{ name, color, usageCount, createdAt }] }
- GET /api/v1/file-types/{name} → get single
  Response: { name, color, usageCount, createdAt }

**Write (admin only):**
- POST /api/v1/file-types → manual creation (optional)
  Request: { name, color }
- PUT /api/v1/file-types/{name} → edit color
  Request: { name, color }
- DELETE /api/v1/file-types/{name} → delete (fails if in use)

**Auto-creation (all users via files API):**
- POST /api/v1/files { fileType: "X" } → auto-creates type X
- PUT /api/v1/files/{name} { fileType: "Y" } → auto-creates type Y

### UI Best Practices

**Defaults Handling:**
- color="" → use UI default (e.g., gray badge)
- Always render badge even with default color

**Type Selector UX:**
- Combobox/autocomplete with type-ahead
- Show existing types with badge preview
- Allow creating new inline (no separate step)
- "Manage types" link to full CRUD UI

**Admin Features:**
- "Orphaned types" filter (usage = 0)
- Bulk delete for cleanup
- Edit any type to add color metadata
- See which files use each type

**Note:** Icon field has been removed from the spec - only color customization is available.

title: Update file API types and service to use fileId instead of name
description:

Changes required in src/types/api.ts:

2. Add UpdateFileResponse: { message: string, fileId: string }

6. Add AvailableFilesResponse type

Changes required in src/services/operatorApi.ts:
1. Update createFile return type to CreateFileResponse
2. Update updateFile return type to UpdateFileResponse
3. Update getFile to use fileId parameter instead of name
4. Update updateFile to use fileId parameter instead of name
5. Update deleteFile to use fileId parameter instead of name
6. Update all JSDoc comments

Changes required in components using file API:
- Update all references from file.name to file.fileId
- Update route parameters from /files/:name to /files/:fileId
- Handle createFile response which now returns fileId instead of full FileResponse

notes:
**Backend API Specification (from docs/api-files-spec.md)**:

POST /api/v1/files → CreateFileResponse { message, fileId }
PUT /api/v1/files/{fileId} → UpdateFileResponse { message, fileId }
GET /api/v1/files/{fileId} → FileResponse { fileId, fileName, content, ... }
GET /api/v1/files/available → AvailableFilesResponse { files: FileInfo[] }
DELETE /api/v1/files/{fileId} → DeleteFileResponse { message }

**Key Changes**:
- User no longer provides 'name' field when creating files
- Backend auto-generates UUID and ConfigMap name (file-<UUID>)
- All endpoints use {fileId} path parameter (UUID string)
- FileResponse.name replaced with FileResponse.fileId

**Testing**:
After changes, test file creation flow:
1. Create file → get fileId from response
2. Use fileId to GET/PUT/DELETE file
3. Verify available files list shows fileId
4. Update routing to use :fileId instead of :name
